mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and lowers equity discount rates, which directly increases present values of future cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, which compresses corporate earnings and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue expansion and generates positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 50% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, which leads to negative short-term S&P 500 returns as investors price in margin erosion.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high CPI periods do not precede negative 5d returns with statistical significance at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPI exceeds 3.0 percent annually, equity valuations face downward pressure as investors anticipate aggressive monetary tightening, which results in negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.0% is followed by positive 20d returns in > 65% of observations over the past decade.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60d returns in > 55% of cases over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion that supports corporate revenue forecasts, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% fails to predict positive 20d returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52% across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 momentum as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: INDPRO MoM increases above 0.5% are followed by negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances over a rolling 3-year evaluation window.
